verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase F1 subunit beta) is a core catalytic subunit of the F1 domain of ATP synthase, assembled on the inner mitochondrial membrane facing the matrix. It is a soluble protein within the F1 complex, not membrane-integrated and not accessible from the extracellular face. The F1 domain sits on the matrix side of the inner membrane; the protein has no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or outer-leaflet lipidation. While ATP synthase is an essential enzyme and ATP5F1B is a structured protein involved in proton-driven ATP synthesis, it does not reach the outer face of the plasma membrane via any mechanism—it is confined to the mitochondrial matrix-side compartment. No cell-state, tissue-restricted, or stress-induced translocation to the PM has been documented.
